steps.title: Substitute x = 49
steps.content:
The notation $f(49)$ means use $x=49$ in the function $f(x)=7+\sqrt{x}$:
$$f(49)=7+\sqrt{49}$$

steps.title: Use the perfect square
steps.content:
$49$ is a perfect square, and $\sqrt{49}=7$. Therefore:
$$f(49)=7+7=14$$
The square root symbol means the nonnegative square root, so use $7$, not $-7$.
